title: The Impact of AI on Society and the Future of Higher Education
content:
The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence is creating profound ripples across modern society, particularly in how we interact with technology. Today, many teenagers consult AI chatbots not only for homework advice, but also for deep moral or philosophical questions that were previously directed at family or faith leaders. However, this growing obsession has sparked concerns, as over-reliance on AI companions has been linked to severe emotional tragedies among youth. Beyond personal use, AI is also driving controversy in public spaces and healthcare. For instance, activists from the Muscogee Nation have protested the use of AI facial recognition, viewing it as a tool for surveillance that disproportionately targets marginalized communities.

In the medical field, hospitals are embracing AI to cut costs, but many nurses report that the tools are unhelpful and sometimes harmful to patient care. While technology continues to disrupt daily life, systemic changes are also being heavily debated in the realm of higher education. Today’s debate over the future of college is often framed as a choice between diversity and meritocracy. However, experts argue that a more crucial question centers on the overwhelming role of wealth in the admissions process. This is underscored by the fact that fewer than 1% of Americans attend elite 'Ivy-Plus' colleges, yet these graduates dominate the nation's top earning and leadership positions.
